title: Implement keystroke capture MVP
description: Build the Go daemon: config loading, event type, JSONL writer, app cache, subscription, handler, main entry point. Plan: docs/superpowers/plans/2026-05-27-it2ks-implementation.md
close_reason: MVP shipped in commits 0abf730..fb9637a; umbrella commit forgot to flip bd state

title: Resolve iTerm2 API magic-cookie auth before smoke test
description: First run of `./it2ks` triggered iTerm2's Python API trust dialog demanding a root-owned magic file (hex-encoded binary path + UUID 61DF88DC-3423-4823-B725-22570E01C027). Dialog path not captured. Two paths forward: (a) re-trigger dialog, capture the file path it names, create file via sudo tee with correct contents+ownership; (b) iTerm2 Prefs → General → Magic → 'Allow all apps to connect' (bypass cookie, looser but fine on single-user machine). Blocks it2ks-tya (advanced=true verification) and it2ks-p3f (Task 11 smoke test) — both need the daemon to actually connect.
close_reason: Bypassed via iTerm2 Prefs → General → Magic → 'Allow all apps to connect' (acceptable on single-user machine). Follow-up filed as it2ks-ls3 to revisit proper file-based trust.

title: Verify iTerm2 honors advanced=true via re-subscribe pattern
description: cmd/it2ks/main.go sends a basic keystroke subscribe via SubscribeToGenericNotifications, then immediately re-subscribes with KeystrokeMonitorRequest{Advanced:true}. Unverified whether iTerm2 actually upgrades the existing subscription on the second request, or whether ALREADY_SUBSCRIBED means the body was ignored. Confirm empirically by observing 'up' events in the log; if absent, may need to unsubscribe-then-subscribe-advanced instead.

title: it2ks manual integration smoke test (Task 11)
description: Run ./it2ks foreground, type in iTerm2 sessions, inspect ~/.it2ks/logs/YYYY-MM-DD.jsonl, validate with scripts/validate-log.go, verify 'up' events appear (advanced=true works), verify app tagging differs per session. Then make install + verify launchd. Plan: docs/superpowers/plans/2026-05-27-it2ks-keystroke-capture.md Task 11.

title: Implement it2ks MVP keystroke capture
description: Track end-to-end build per docs/superpowers/plans/2026-05-27-it2ks-keystroke-capture.md. 11 tasks: bootstrap, config, event, filter, appcache, writer, capture loop, main wiring, validator, launchd, smoke test.
close_reason: MVP code complete: tasks 1-10. 13 unit tests pass. Review fixes applied (single time.Now per event, robust closeLocked, AppCache concurrency docs). Task 11 manual smoke moved to its own issue.

title: Revisit iTerm2 magic-cookie auth (proper file-based trust)
description: Follow-up to it2ks-8hq. For MVP we bypassed via iTerm2 Prefs → General → Magic → 'Allow all apps to connect'. That's fine on dk's single-user machine, but the proper path is: re-trigger the trust dialog, capture the magic-file path it names, create the file via sudo tee with correct contents+ownership (UUID 61DF88DC-3423-4823-B725-22570E01C027 + hex-encoded binary path). Do this before any multi-user or distribution scenario.
